title: Reusable Components: Organizing Header, Footer, and Navigation as Separate Template Files

content.problemStatement:
In this lesson, we'll learn how to create reusable components in Flask by organizing header, footer, and navigation as separate template files. \
This technique allows us to:
1. Keep our code DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself)
2. Maintain consistency across pages
3. Easily update shared components in one place

Here's how we can organize our templates:

1. Create a 'components' folder inside your 'templates' directory.

2. Create separate files for each component:
   - header.html
   - footer.html
   - navigation.html

3. In each component file, add the relevant HTML code. \
For example, in header.html:

   ```html
   
     My Flask App
   
   ```

4. In your base template or individual page templates, you can now include these components using Jinja2's include statement:

   ```html
   {% include 'components/header.html' %}
   {% include 'components/navigation.html' %}
   
   
   
   {% include 'components/footer.html' %}
   ```

By organizing your templates this way, you can easily maintain and update shared components across your entire Flask application. \
If you need to change the header, you only need to modify the header.html file, and the changes will be reflected on all pages that include it.

Remember, this approach works well with the URL routing we learned earlier. \
You can still use your route decorators in your Flask application to render these modular templates for different URLs.
